applied_optimizations.name: Triton depthwise conv2d kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented Triton kernel for depthwise 2D convolution with asymmetric kernel (3x7). Uses 3D grid (batch, channel, flattened_output_position) with block size 256. Each thread block processes BLOCK_SIZE output positions, looping over kernel elements (3x7=21 multiplies per output). Properly handles boundary conditions with masking. Uses nn.Conv2d internally for weight management to ensure identical initialization to reference.

attempted_but_failed.name: 2D grid with height blocking
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incorrect handling of boundary conditions when out_height is not a multiple of BLOCK_SIZE_H

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual weight initialization
attempted_but_failed.reason: Could not match PyTorch's exact random number consumption pattern

notes: Triton kernel achieves 3.14x speedup over PyTorch reference (0.624ms vs 1.96ms)
Key insight: Use flattened output position grid to simplify boundary handling
Must use nn.Conv2d internally for weight management to match reference initialization exactly
BLOCK_SIZE=256 works well for this problem size (32*128*126*250 = ~127M output elements)
